title: Nemia angolensis
intro: Nemia angolensis, also known as the Angolan Nemia, is a plant species belonging to the family Polypodiaceae, commonly found in tropical regions of Africa. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, with reported health benefits including the treatment of fever, rheumatism, and gastrointestinal issues. The bioactive constituents of Nemia angolensis include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Traditionally, Nemia angolensis is prepared as a decoction or infusion, and is often used in folk medicine to make teas, tinctures, and infusions for the treatment of various ailments.
taxonomy: Nemia angolensis belongs to the family Arecaceae and is classified within the genus Nemia. The species is native to Angola, and its classification is used to distinguish it from other palm species in the region.
